prompt: Camera move left. A broad alpine valley in late autumn, with dark spruce forests climbing the lower slopes and pale granite walls rising beyond them. Fresh snow dusts the upper ridges while the valley floor remains wet and brown with thawed grass and scattered stones. A braided river threads through the center, its cold surface broken by riffles and small gravel bars. Low storm clouds are clearing from one side of the sky, revealing a band of amber evening light that travels across the terrain. Mist clings to the far cliffs, and intermittent gusts comb rain across the river, changing the sheen of water and rock.

Camera move right. An eroded desert badlands basin at first light, arranged in layered depth with cracked clay foreground, tilted sediment fins, and tall buttes stepping back toward a distant escarpment. The ground shows distinct alluvial channels carved by flash floods, with scattered talus at the base of the steeper walls. Bands of ochre, ash gray, and iron red mark the exposed strata, each ledge catching a different intensity of dawn. Thin veils of dust drift along the basin floor after a dry wind, while sparse sagebrush and hardy grasses cling to shallow pockets where runoff briefly collects after rare storms.

Camera orbit left. A circular glacial lake enclosed by steep rock and conifer slopes, with a low shelf of rounded boulders in the foreground leading into exceptionally clear water. Near the shore, submerged stones and wavering aquatic plants remain visible beneath the surface, then the basin deepens to a dark blue center that mirrors broken cloud and high peaks. A narrow waterfall enters from one side, sending ripples outward in overlapping rings and throwing fine spray onto nearby moss. Small wind shifts wrinkle the reflections unevenly, while patches of lingering snow and damp granite emphasize the cold, high-elevation air.

Camera orbit right. A lonely coastal headland in early winter, where stratified sea cliffs descend to a black pebble beach and a natural sea arch stands just offshore. The tide is rising, with long gray-green swells folding into white water around the rock stacks and surging through narrow channels cut into the shore platform. Above, a dense ceiling of cloud is breaking apart after rain, opening strips of cold sunlight that ignite parts of the wet cliff face while leaving others in blue shadow. Salt haze softens the far horizon, beach grass bends under gusts, and drifting showers still hang over the distant water.
